transcript.text: Hello.
How's it going?
Rachel, were you just off for Thanksgiving?
You're in the U.S., right?
Right.
Yeah.
Happy Thanksgiving.
Yeah, I hope you have a nice, it's always like, I always come back from Thanksgiving wanting to just eat like just soup or something.
So everything's so heavy.
Like a salad, a vegetable.
Yeah, I need to just really keep it light for a little bit.
Hi, Fatima.
It's nice to meet you.
Hi, likewise.
Where are you guys from?
I am based in the U.S.
on the East Coast.
I'm originally from New York City, but now I live in Vermont, which if you're not familiar with U.S.
geography, most people haven't heard of it.
But it's like a very rural kind of mountainous state up near the Canadian border.
No, I actually did like the state-wise pages for Intervel, so I didn't know the state.
That's funny.
Yeah.
All right, well, think.
think.
And Fatima, where are you based?
Are you based in Pakistan?
Pakistan.
Yep.
Okay.
And did you just, you just got back from maternity leave, right?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Congratulations.
Thank you.
congrats.
Welcome back.
Thank you.
All right.
Just curious, what is growthx?
they have, like, is it a good maternity leave or does it, like, on a personal, like, case-by-case basis or, like, how does that work?
Um, it was, like, a 16-week maternity leave, because that's, that's what you're asking yet.
Yeah.
Oh, that's pretty good.
That's nice.
I feel like sometimes, like, startups or small companies, they can, it, like, varies so much.
I wasn't like, expecting yet, like, 16 weeks, but it was, it was great.
Yeah.
when I had to come back, I was, like, um, I could have, you know, been happier if I'd get, like, maybe more time off.
Yeah.
He's still.
Yeah, it's still so small.
Yeah.
Yeah, I know.
It's weird.
It's so different.
Like, was in Canada when I had one of my kids.
And there, it's like a year of leave.
Yeah, yeah, I'm weird.
Whoa.
Go, Canada.
Hi, Kelvin.
Okay.
So I wanted to have everyone here just to talk a little bit through both Relay and Otto, the clients, and then also kind of talking about roles and priorities for kind of this week and going forward.
Um, I think the plan is, um, for Fatima to take over, um, generating the articles.
Um, but I think, Rachel, because, uh, Tiro is churning, the hope is that at

the beginning, you would be sort of available to maybe help with some editing at the beginning, I think, was Andy's thought if Fatima needed support while she's kind of, like, getting up and running and used to the new systems, which is why I wanted you here, too, just so you have, like, a little bit more context on the clients in case you get pulled in to help.
And then Kelvin is going to help make sure the handoff documents are updated and close out.
We still have some content from last week that hadn't been delivered yet.
So he's going to also help close out all that content to make sure we're kind of maintaining our velocity here.
So my thought was I wanted to check in with Fatima and Rachel, with both of you, and see what your comfort was with things like...
Keyword research with updating the artifacts and, like, being, like, working within Atlas.
Fatima, I know it probably looks a lot different than, you know, 16 weeks ago.
I imagine the systems have changed a lot.
So I'm curious, you know, what is your comfort with things like updating artifacts in Atlas?
Because that may be somewhere where we could pull in Rachel for a little bit of help, which might be more useful than even editing after.
Yeah, think I haven't really worked on updating the artifacts yet.
So I would love to, like, still sit back and see how it's done until, like, I'm really, I don't want to mess things up for anyone.
So, yeah.
I mean, I think you can create a copy of the pipeline, which is what a lot of people are doing.
And that way you don't have to worry about that.
Because I think in some ways, the more you learn, like, the voice and tone and what's not going well in the...
Like, you're usually the person, like, well-situated to make those updates.
So I actually think that, you know, working, talking to Kelvin, too, about what he's been seeing and what he's been taking care of in post-processing, I think, would be capturing that.
And then I sent you that chat we had with Hassan.
Yeah, yeah.

Yeah, so I think the biggest thing he found was that making sure that the artifacts are written in the actual voice and tone that you want for that client, I think he saw a really big change in the quality of the output there.
So I think updating them to reflect that, I think, is where he's seen the biggest change.

And I'm flagging this because I think, and honestly, this will be useful, too, because I think we're having a sometimes similar problem with the hyper-exponential, is that I think both Auto and Relay recently moved on to the agentic pipeline.
And so I think we have been getting not great results, especially for Auto.
So I think.
think that focusing on updating those artifacts at the beginning is going to save a lot of kind of work later on.
So I think that that could be something that would be worthwhile working with Kelvin on this week to try to make a copy of that pipeline and work on updating those.
Okay.
So in terms of Auto, just to give you sort of an overview of the clients, I sent you sort of the operating manual, but just to get a sense of them.
So Auto is a travel and AI travel assistant for business travels specifically.
So it's an app that can help business travelers who don't have sort of, who usually work at a startup or a small company where they're booking their own travel.
It will help them do their flights and their hotels.
It can integrate with their calendars so they can make sure they have enough buffer time between.
meetings.
And you can, it'll track their flights.
So if there's a delay, it can kind of help you rebook your flight all through a conversational interface.
So it kind of streamlines the whole business travel process.
It's very new.
So they haven't really had all of their blog content we've done for them.
So they're sort of still getting up and running.
And we have a good runway of content and their content OS of approved article topics.
So I think in terms of choosing article topics, that's something I can help you with.
But I think in general, just like choosing one, you know, hitting each of the topic clusters each week is basically what I have been doing.
And then sometimes the client will go in and they will leave a comment on them in the air table if there's something they want to like particularly prioritize next.
In terms of an overview of the pipeline, I think the things that aren't working is

We're getting really choppy sentences, and I think this is the biggest thing that updating the artifacts, because most of the artifacts are written with very choppy sentences and no transitions between them to try and keep them short.
But what we found out is that that means we're getting articles with really choppy sentences that all need to be fixed and clawed post-processing.
So I think that's definitely true here.
We're getting articles that need better transitions.

And I think the biggest things that I'm seeing that are of concern is we did build a product features artifact in Atlas, but it still seems to be making mistakes and making up new features for auto that don't exist.

So I think, A, that's something to think about in terms of we should be updating the artifacts, but I think for now, especially as you're less familiar with the product itself, I think it's worth making sure you do like a fact-checking step.

And Claude just confirming that the features exist, that they're kind of positioned in the correct way, because despite the artifact being up to date in Atlas, as of last week, we're still kind of getting mistakes there.
And then I think the other thing to check is I found with Auto, we're getting links to not competitors in terms of like competing products, but sort of mindshare competitors.
So it's linking to articles that are clearly like aiming for the same keyword.
So I think checking, making sure you check the links to make sure that they're kind of accurate.
And this is something I've seen across agentic pipelines is they're not always great at choosing what to link to.
So it's something worth asking around to see who's getting really good results.
And I don't know, Rachel, you might know, based on your pipelines, to see how that stage is like controlled in the pipeline.
Because I feel like for both.
With auto and relay, I think we're not getting awesome results there.
Yeah, I usually add in like in the agentic pipelines, there's usually a like internal link thing to target.
And I think if you make sure that that's to the blog and not just the website, like most pipelines just link to the website.
And then from there you get like wide spanning results versus if you just do like auto.com slash like resources slash blog or something, it generally narrows it down.
And then I always add in a step in like the instructions or directions that say like do not link to anything but this or something like that, you know, because yeah, it's not great.
good at like the context because I find sometimes it'll like do the internal linking, but it like doesn't make any sense based on the like it's just the wrong article to link to.
It's just not that.
Yeah, I do feel like it's still not great.
Like you definitely have to kind of manually go through.
And yeah, check everything, which is irritating.
But I don't know that anyone's found like a perfect workaround yet, or like a good automation step.
You know, I think we're all kind of still having to like manually spend 10 minutes to check everything.
Well, good to know.
Yeah, it's not good news, but All right.
Anyone have any questions about auto specifically?
Otherwise, I'll just run through Relay as well quickly.
Okay.
And so Relay is a banking platform that is focused on banking for small businesses.
So they serve clients like right now, they're very focused on home services.
So like, we're doing a lot of blog, like accounting for like your plumbing business, how to like manage your cash flow for other small businesses.

And they're also kind of wanting to own the AI for small businesses space, like AI apps that small business owners can use to manage their small businesses, because I think they feel like there's not a lot of content focused on that audience.
And that's an audience that probably doesn't naturally gravitate towards AI tools that might be more resistant to trusting them.
So you'll see we have a lot of topic clusters for that one, I think, because we keep adding on, like we added on the AI for small businesses.
And this week, I am, they've handed off some documentation, and I'm going to be doing a little bit of strategy and research around this new, like, accounting and bookkeeping cluster.
They want to get started.
So same thing there.
There's a pretty good runway of content.
They tend to be, they tend to approve smaller batches of articles.
So I'm often going back and asking them to come.
But I think we've got their approving articles for this week, and then we're going to hand off these new keywords.
So I think one place I would need support this week is probably generating some new assignments based on the keyword research for this client.
So I haven't dug into the documentation they handed off.
I'll see how straightforward it is, whether I can just hand off the keywords themselves or if it seems like they might need more context.
So I wanted to know, Fatima, what's your sort of comfort with keyword research, with kind of like generating article topics and updating Airtable?
Is that something that you did before?
Yeah, I've done both, so I'm like comfortable with whatever you're assigning me because I actually personally love keyword research.
Oh, great.
Yeah.
Okay, well, that's great.
Well, then I'll see at the very least, I'm going to come up with.
Like seed keywords and some direction based on what they handed off.
But my thought, my thinking is based on my conversation with them that it's going to be an expansion of what we have rather than something totally different.
So I will dig into that hopefully today or tomorrow morning and then I can kind of just keep you updated based on that.
And in terms of how the pipeline, I think the relay pipeline in general is doing a better job on a more granular level.
I think it's sometimes repeating concepts.
I find it's going too far with voice and tone.
Like they like the sort of more conversational voice and tone, but it'll sort of go straight to these conversational examples without telling you what they're for.
Like it won't say, you know, the article is about accounting best practices.
It'll jump straight to like, it's 7 a.m.
and your app is uploaded for the fifth time, you know, and then it's like you get the same kind of like way to like.
It's like all tone with no substance, I would say.
So I think drawing it back a little bit sometimes in post-processing is something that we have been doing.
Same thing, I think sometimes the internal links are really like barely related, and that's something that the client has flagged for us before.
So just another one to make sure we're checking those kind of internal links as we go.

So, but otherwise, I think if you read through those handoff docs, Kelvin's going to help update anything that's changed with the agentic pipeline, but hopefully those will give you a good sense of where we are.
And then I think if you're generating for auto and relay this week, know, Kelvin will be available, and obviously I'll be available to, like, answer questions and leave feedback, too.
Awesome.
Okay.
So then in terms of...

My thought was, Kelvin, if you could focus on cleaning, like, just all that content from last week, making sure that all gets updated, delivered to the client, making sure that anything with outstanding feedback gets closed out on that kind of, like, bullet point list of stuff from last week, that that all gets closed out.

So, Fatima, if you want to start generating articles for Auto and Relay, and then, you know, you could do one or two for each client, and then I think we could either put time on our calendar with you and Kelvin or the three of us to kind of talk through anything that's not working or questions you have.

And then I think thinking about how to update the artifacts, if you sort of, there's some stuff in Notion about updating them, I think it's worth watching that meeting with Hassan to see how he thinks about it.
And I think that might be, I think.
It's going to be something that's worth doing in the next week or two as well.
Okay.
And then, yeah, Rachel, think maybe we'll just pull you in.
Like, I'm wondering, is updating the artifacts something that you feel, like, comfortable and something that you were working on with your other clients?
Or is that something mostly that the ME was handling?
Because I know every pod was a little bit different.
Yeah, it kind of varied, like, based on the different clients.
So, like, I would handle one usually.
Or maybe, like, we would kind of alternate and, like, update things as we went.
So if you guys want help updating the artifacts, I can probably, like, jump in and provide some guidance or, like, help with the process and stuff.
I think, like, it would be, if I do jump in and help, it would be helpful if Fatima and I did it, like, together.
Because I sometimes think when somebody else does
Is it on something that you are mostly working on?
can kind of like get messy.
So I like, I'd love to help out or like do it maybe as like a collaborative effort.
Cause I think that process might be more beneficial for like the next time you have to update the artifact.
Yeah.
Cause my thought is, think Andy had mentioned you as editing support, but I actually think that if we can get better results from the artifacts, then, you know, you'll be less editing support.
And, you I feel like it'll just be better for everyone long-term.
So I'm thinking if we pull you in to support this week, that might be a place that makes more sense than.
Yeah, for sure.
And editing the articles.
Okay.
So maybe that makes sense.
And then I think the question is, I guess, based, Rachel, based on like how you are working, like, what do you feel like you're banned with?
mean, cause you'll be working on Tiro this week.
So I'm assuming that you'll be pretty busy.
So I think then it's just a question of your bandwidth and, like, your availability support.
So we could just change your space, like, in a few days.
yeah, I don't know, because I don't know how much, like, logic and hyper-exponential are going to require.
And then I have a full, like, whatever, we're wrapping up Tiro.
So I can let you know, I guess, maybe, like, as I get into it.
helping you prioritize your workload.
Logic is going to be, I will update.
Basically, I'm meeting with them.
They're thinking about a shift in strategy.
So I think we're going to be on pause for them, at least for Monday and Tuesday.
So Wednesday, I'm going to sync up with them and figure out how they want to execute.
So I think whatever work we have for them this week will come at the end of the week.
And for hyper-exponential, I also have to share, I think, share some new article topics with them based on new research and documentation that we got last week.
So I have to close that out today.

So it might be worth you and I also syncing because I think what we'll do is they have this new stream of comparison content that might be worth us running, I think, one or two articles sort of as calibration because we're changing how we talk about their product.
So we may just be doing more calibration this week.
We may not be running the full five.
So it actually might be a good week for you, at least at the beginning of the week, too, if you have more availability to pop in and help with the artifacts.
Yeah, yeah, that sounds good because I think if we're not doing much for those two, then all I've really got going is Tiro.
So I could definitely pick up some other stuff and drop in.
Awesome.
Okay, so maybe then in terms of artifact generally, working on the artifacts, maybe you and Fatima could make some time earlier this week to take a look at those.
Does that make sense?
Yeah, for sure.
Okay.
Yeah.
Was there anything...
Kelvin, don't know, did you want to walk through your process for how you're talking, like how you sort of get articles set up for auto and relay or like post-processing?
Or do you feel like that's all kind of pretty captured in the handoff documents?
Well, I think the handoff document hasn't changed much.
The process that used to be there is quite similar to what I'm doing right now.
But I can try to maybe add, as you said, what's different now in that document notion.
Okay.
That's okay.
Because I haven't looked at the documents yet today, so I wasn't sure how much you had updated them last week at the end of the week when I was off.
No, I didn't get time to do that, unfortunately, last week, but I was planning to do that.
They have to edit some content that was done last week.
So I'm prioritizing that, then I'll get to the documents, to one of the documents.
Okay.
Then maybe, just because I don't want to block Fatima on creating new content, like running new, getting set up on running new articles for this week.
Um, so maybe it's worth, um, you talking through, like your process, at least?
Oh, I'm going to pull my hand.
It's better when I'm reaching down.
Is that okay?
Okay, that's fine.
So if you can update those.
Um, so I guess then, Fatima, maybe today and tomorrow, you could, um, at the beginning that we could
would focus on the updating the artifacts and then Kelvin can update those handoff documents after he's done delivering the content from last week.
Okay.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
That makes sense.
Okay.
And did you guys, Fatima or Rachel, did you guys have any more questions for now?
I'm sure there'll be a lot of stuff that comes up and we'll just have to sort of, you know, I'm always happy to hop on and talk live or, you know, we can do stuff in Slack async too.
Yeah.
I'll go through everything now, like look at the client's feedback and all and talk to Kelvin about what's not working and then we'll see where it goes.
Yeah.
And if you want to run, like I know technically I'm not editing anymore, but I think especially at the beginning while you're getting up to speed, like if you would like to run one or two.

articles and have me go through and just leave feedback on them, I'm happy to do that, too, because I think that can be very useful for updating the artifacts, too, just to have feedback to work with.
Yeah, yeah, that would help.
Okay, that sounds good.
Well, then I'll give everyone the last few minutes of their time back.
All right.
Bye, everyone.
Thank you.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To align the team on client priorities and roles for the week.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=265.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Fatima now owns article generation for Otto & Relay.** Rachel will provide collaborative support on artifact updates, not editing, to improve pipeline quality.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
  - [**Top priority: Fix poor agentic pipeline output.** The root cause is artifacts written for brevity, which produce choppy sentences and factual errors (e.g., Otto's AI inventing features).](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
  - [**Kelvin will first deliver last week's content,** then update handoff docs. Fatima will generate 1–2 articles per client for Bailey to review, providing immediate feedback for artifact improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)

## Topics

### Problem: Poor Agentic Pipeline Output

  - [The recent shift to the agentic pipeline for Otto & Relay has degraded article quality, creating significant post-processing work.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
  - [**Root Cause:** Artifacts are written for brevity, not natural language. This produces choppy sentences and poor transitions in the final articles.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=593.0)
  - [**Key Issues:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=593.0)
      - [**Factual Errors (Otto):** The AI invents non-existent product features, requiring a manual fact-checking step.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
      - [**Tone Mismatch (Relay):** The AI overuses conversational examples ("7 a.m. and your app is uploaded...") without providing context, resulting in "all tone, no substance."](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=981.0)
      - [**Irrelevant Internal Links:** Links often go to "mindshare competitors" (articles targeting the same keywords) or are contextually wrong.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=676.0)
          - [**Fix:** Restrict the pipeline's link-target URL to the client's blog (e.g., `auto.com/resources/blog`) and add an explicit "do not link to anything but this" instruction.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=724.0)

### Solution: Collaborative Artifact Updates

  - [**Rationale:** Improving artifact quality is the most effective way to fix the pipeline's output and reduce manual editing.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1219.0)
  - [**Process:** Fatima and Rachel will collaborate on artifact updates early this week.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
      - [**Why:** Collaboration ensures a shared understanding of the process, which is more beneficial for long-term ownership than Rachel simply editing articles.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1190.0)
      - [**Action:** Fatima will create a copy of the pipeline to safely test changes.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=404.0)
  - [**Guidance:** Review the Notion meeting with Hassan, who confirmed that artifacts written in the client's actual voice and tone dramatically improve output quality.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=437.0)

### Client Context & Priorities

  - [**Otto:** AI travel assistant for business travelers.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=509.0)
      - [**Content Focus:** A new product with a large runway of approved topics.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
      - [**Priority:** Fact-checking all product features and links.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
  - [**Relay:** Banking platform for small businesses.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=809.0)
      - [**Content Focus:** Home services accounting and "AI for small businesses" (to own this niche).](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=815.0)
      - [**New Project:** Bailey is researching a new "accounting and bookkeeping" cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0)
      - [**Action:** Fatima will conduct keyword research and generate assignments for this new cluster once Bailey provides seed keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=906.0)

### Team Roles & Weekly Plan

  - [**Kelvin:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
      - [**Priority 1:** Deliver all outstanding content from last week.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
      - [**Priority 2:** Update the handoff documents in Notion to reflect current agentic pipeline processes.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
  - [**Fatima:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
      - [**Priority 1:** Collaborate with Rachel on artifact updates.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
      - [**Priority 2:** Generate 1–2 articles per client for Bailey's feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1550.0)
      - [**Priority 3:** Conduct keyword research for Relay's new cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=906.0)
  - [**Rachel:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
      - [**Priority 1:** Collaborate with Fatima on artifact updates.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1146.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This is feasible due to lighter workloads for Logic (paused Mon/Tues) and Hyper-Exponential (calibration articles only).](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1244.0)
  - [**Bailey:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1059.0)
      - [**Priority 1:** Provide feedback on Fatima's initial articles to guide artifact improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1550.0)
      - [**Priority 2:** Provide seed keywords to Fatima for Relay's new cluster.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=906.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Fatima & Rachel:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1363.0)
      - [Schedule a collaborative session early this week to update Otto & Relay artifacts.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1363.0)
  - [**Fatima:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
      - [Generate 1–2 articles per client for Bailey's review.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1550.0)
      - [Conduct keyword research for Relay's new cluster upon receiving seed keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=906.0)
  - [**Kelvin:**](https://fathom.video/share/ob1jhWndLEYA3MbZHeuk38ndZePKAS2-?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
